title: pilosella subulatissima
intro: Pilosella subulatissima, also known as the needle-leaved mouse-ear hawk's-beard, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation, due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, and it may also have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Pilosella subulatissima is typically prepared as a tincture or infusion, and it can be consumed as a herbal remedy, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
